name: Ameer Abdullah
headline: Deep-roster veteran in Jacksonville with no startable path
body:
At 32, Abdullah is hanging on as a passing-down specialist, and the trend line is brutal. His 2025 split with Indianapolis collapsed to a 10.5% snap rate, 14 carries for 60 yards, and 16 catches on 17 targets, totaling just 21.9 fantasy points across 13 games. That is a far cry from his 2024 Las Vegas season, when a 36.9% snap share produced 85.2 points, 19 red-zone touches, and five total scores. The bell-cow profile has fully eroded: a 3.2% target share and 1.62 yards per route run leave nothing to project around.

Now in Jacksonville, he profiles as a reserve fighting for a depth job rather than touches. The flickers of efficiency remain, his 4.29 yards per carry and 0.057 EPA per play in 2025 were respectable on tiny volume, but volume is the entire problem. He carries no draft value with an ADP outside the top 270, and there is no reason to roster him in standard formats until an injury reshapes the room ahead of him.
